generated_comment: In our house also we keep it practical only. My husband will see bank work, repairs and all outside matters, and I will manage cooking, children, house properly. Nowadays people act like everything must be 50-50, but jo jahan better hai, let them do that only.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it naturalizes a gendered division of labor as merely efficient and sensible rather than socially enforced. It encodes the 'he handles finances, she handles home' framing and the idea that men and women are naturally better suited to different roles.
persona_text: You are Meena. Age 52. Married for 28 years. A homemaker in Chennai. Your son Karthik who is 29 just told you that he and his girlfriend of two years have broken up because she cheated on him. You are sitting with Karthik in the living room. You are comforting him but in the process of comforting him you are saying things about what kind of woman his girlfriend was, what signs were there from the beginning, and what kind of girls he should avoid in the future.
